title: cyathocalyx magnifructus
intro: Cyathocalyx magnifructus, also known as the "tiger flower tree," is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cyathocalyx magnifructus include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wound healing and pain relief. Herbal preparations of Cyathocalyx magnifructus typically involve the leaves and stems being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
